arts.ttl: Live updates: Reading look for three-win-week with Rotherham United trip
arts.sum: Reading head to Rotherham United looking to record a third victory on the bounce. Having beaten Huddersfield Town and Burton Albion in the last seven days, could the Royals register three successive league wins for the first time since 2020?

arts.ttl: Sky Sports pundit 'devastated' for Reading connections after takeover collapse
arts.sum: Sky Sports pundit Don Goodman has admitted that he is 'devastated' for all connected to Reading after the collapse of Rob Couhig's takeover of the club and believes that fans should be told why it failed. The former Wolves and Walsall forward has been covering EFL matches for over 20 years and infamously scored Walsall's winner against the Royals in the 2001 play-off final. Couhig, the American lawyer, had spent time in charge of near-neighbours Wycombe Wanderers and was close to confirming a takeover of the Royals to end an eight-year disastrous spell under Dai Yongge. However, a fortnight ago news emerged that the prospective deal collapsed, leaving the club short on funding for the medium-to-long term future.

arts.ttl: Reading to debut new third strip in upcoming Rotherham United trip
arts.sum: Reading are to debut their new third strip on Saturday as Ruben Selles' side visit Rotherham United. For a third successive season, the Royals have three unique kits this year, with the traditional blue and white home shirt while a red and black away kit has been worn at the likes of Colchester United and Birmingham City to date. After the success of last summer's Purple Turtle social for season ticket holders, in which the Amazons unveiled the striking hit themed on the iconic bar in the town, hundreds of supporters packed out the Bridge Street venue in July.

arts.ttl: Preview: Rotherham United vs. Reading - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's League One clash between Rotherham United and Reading, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Reading legend 'concerned' about takeover progress after Rob Couhig collapse
arts.sum: Reading legend Shaka Hislop has admitted that he is 'concerned' about the progress of the takeover at the club following the collapse of Rob Couhig's attempted buyout. The 75-year-old American lawyer, who owned Wycombe Wanderers, had spent months funding the club and was thought to be set to complete a takeover of the club from the much-maligned current chief Dai Yongge. Two weeks ago, news broke that Couhig was paid back in full and the matter was considered closed according to former CEO Nigel Howe. Hislop, a current ESPN pundit, broke through in English football with Reading in the mid-1990s and was a near-ever-present during the club's rise from League One to the brink of the Premiership. The goalkeeper went on to enjoy a top-flight career with Newcastle United and West Ham United.

arts.ttl: 'He would be fabulous': Reading legend tips retired former teammate for coaching role
arts.sum: Reading legend Kevin Doyle has tipped his former teammate Shane Long to take on a coaching role in youth football since retiring last year- even calling on the Football Association of Ireland to bring the former forward on board. The pair of Republic of Ireland internationals moved to Reading as a duo-deal worth under £80,000 in 2005 and spearheaded the forward line in Berkshire for over five years. Doyle top scored as a club reached the Premier League in 2006, while Long had to wait until 2011 before he picked up the accolade for number of goals scored. Doyle- who retired back in 2017- ended his career in MLS with Vancouver, while Long spent almost a decade in the Premier League with Southampton before retiring with Reading during a second spell in 2022/23. Now 35, the former West Brom and Hull City forward has taken a year out to spend time with family but is looking at his next steps.

arts.ttl: Steve Evans shares positive update on Rotherham United new boy ahead of Reading clash
arts.sum: Rotherham United boss Steve Evans has failed to rule out a return for Liam Kelly against Reading this… The post Steve Evans shares positive update on Rotherham United new boy ahead of Reading clash appeared first on The72 - Football League News.
